content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 31, 2011

Anthony J. Portantino
Assemblymember 44th District
State Capitol
P O Box 942849
Sacramento CA 94249-0044

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our file No. A-11-162

Honorable Assembly Member Portantino:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the "personal use" provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 In addition, this letter is based on the facts
presented. The Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as the
finder of fact when it renders advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)

QUESTION

Does the Act prohibit you from using your campaign committee funds to retain legal
counsel and pay related costs incurred in your efforts to compel the disclosure and release of
financial documents from the State Assembly and the Assembly Rules Committee?

CONCLUSION

No. Based on the facts as described, the Act does not prohibit you from using funds
from your campaign committee to pay for attorney's fees and other costs related to your
efforts to compel the release of financial information from the State Assembly and Assembly
Rules Committee because the action arises directly out of your activities, duties, or status as a
candidate or elected officer.

_______________
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code
of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations,
unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-11-162
Page No. 2

FACTS

You are an Assembly member representing the 44th District in the California State
Legislature. You are requesting formal written advice regarding the use of campaign funds
to compel the California State Assembly to release documents and writings.

You were provided with a letter on July 7, 2011 from the Assembly Rules Committee
that stated that office expenses for the 44th Assembly District were exceeding the allowed
budget as established by the Rules Committee. As a "corrective measure," the Assembly
Rules Committee informed you that it would furlough the staff of the 44th Assembly District
for a period of 40 days starting October 21, 2011.

You have requested from the Assembly Rules Committee all writings and
documentation regarding Assembly budgeting and expenditures in order to ascertain whether
the information provided to you is accurate. You have yet to receive any of the requested
information.

You wish to know whether you may use campaign funds to retain legal counsel and
to pay other reasonably related costs incurred to compel disclosure and release of financial
information, including but not limited to, operational budgets, expenditures, and related
documents from the State Assembly and Assembly Rules Committee.

ANALYSIS

In general, laws relating to the "personal use" of campaign funds were designed to
prevent candidates, elected officials, and others who control the expenditure of campaign
funds from benefiting privately from their campaign activities. The general rule is that an
expenditure of campaign funds must be reasonably related to a political, legislative or
governmental purpose. However, where an expenditure of campaign funds confers a
substantial personal benefit on any individual or individuals with authority to approve the
expenditure of campaign funds held by the committee, the expenditure must be directly
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. (Section 89512.5.)

In addition, Section 89513(b) states that campaign funds may not be used to
reimburse the cost of professional services unless the services are directly related to a
political, legislative, or governmental purpose. "Expenditures by a committee to pay for
professional services reasonably required by the committee to assist it in the performance of
its administrative functions are directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose." (Section 89513(b)(2).)

The Act contains specific provisions governing the use of campaign funds. (Sections
89510-89522.) These sections are generally referred to as the "personal use of campaign
funds" provisions.

File No. A-11-162
Page No. 3


Attorney's Fees

Section 89514 applies specifically to payment of attorney's fees:

Expenditures of campaign funds for attorney's fees and other costs in
connection with administrative, civil, or criminal litigation are not
directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose
except where the litigation is directly related to activities of a
committee that are consistent with its primary objectives or arises
directly out of a committee's activities or out of a candidate's or
elected officer's activities, duties, or status as a candidate or elected
officer, including, but not limited to, an action to enjoin defamation,
defense of an action to enjoin defamation, defense of an action
brought for a violation of state or local campaign, disclosure, or
election laws, and an action arising from an election contest or
recount. (Emphasis added.)

An expenditure is directly related to a candidate or officeholder's "activities, duties,
or status" if there is a direct relationship between the purpose of the expenditure and the
office sought or held by the public official. (In re Montoya (1989) 12 FPPC Ops. 7.)

Thus, under Section 89514, the Commission has advised that campaign funds may be
expended for legal advice prior to commencement of an action (Richter Advice Letter, No. I-
93-355); campaign funds may be expended for defense of a conflict-of-interest charge
(Lanning Advice Letter, No. A-92-050); and, campaign funds may be used in defense of a
complaint lodged with the Federal Elections Commission even though it is not one of the
actions enumerated in the statute for which campaign funds may be used. (Bagatelos Advice
Letter, No. A-94-091.) Additionally, under section 89513(c)(2), campaign funds may be
expended for the payment of fines arising from violations of state or local campaign,
disclosure, or election laws.

However, campaign funds may not be used to defend against charges that do not arise
directly out of the candidate or officeholder's duties, activities, or status as a candidate or
officeholder. For example, we have advised that a council member who was accused of
falsifying a city government health insurance document could not use campaign
contributions for his legal defense because this action was not related to his status as a public
official, but would relate to any employee with health insurance. (Breitfelder Advice Letter,
No. A-95-058.)

Your facts indicate that you wish to use campaign funds to retain legal counsel and
pay for legal and other related fees in connection with your efforts to compel the State
Assembly and the Assembly Rules Committee to disclose financial documents that would
shed light on the budget and expenses for the 44th Assembly District. This information is
necessary because the Assembly Rules Committee claims your office is over-budget and is

File No. A-11-162
Page No. 4


requiring a "corrective measure" to furlough all staff of the 44th Assembly District for 40
days—a substantial period of time that would have significant impact on the office you hold.

The ultimate purpose of the legal expenses is to keep your office accessible to your
constituents, and allow you to continue representation of the 44th Assembly District. This
purpose furthers your committee's primary objective, which is to maintain your public office
and allow you to continue representing your district. It also arises directly out of your status
as a public official.

We find there is a direct relation between the purpose of the expenditure, the office
you hold, and your status as a public official. Therefore, the expenditures for attorney's fees
and related expenses associated with this action are permissible uses of campaign funds.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Zackery P. Morazzini
General Counsel

By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division

ER:jgl
